name: Bibimbap (Korean Rice Bowl)
content:
# Bibimbap (Korean Rice Bowl)

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/bibimbap/)
**Cuisine:** korean
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 30 min
**Cook Time:** 20 min
**Total Time:** 50 min
**Yield:** 4 servings

## Description
A rice bowl topped with seasoned sautéed vegetables, marinated beef, a sunny-side-up fried egg, sesame seeds, and a generous dollop of sweet-spicy-savoury Bibimbap sauce. Mix it all together before eating!

## Bibimbap Sauce (The Star)
- 4 tbsp gochujang paste
- 2 tbsp mirin
- 2 tbsp rice vinegar
- 1.5 tsp soy sauce (light/all-purpose)
- 3 tsp white sugar
- 1 clove garlic, finely grated
- 2.5 tsp toasted sesame oil

## Marinated Beef
- 250g / 8oz beef tenderloin or thick steak, very finely sliced
- ¼ green apple, grated
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp ginger, minced
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp honey
- 2 tsp toasted sesame oil

## Vegetables (Cooked Separately)
- **Dried shiitake mushrooms** (8 large) — soaked 30 min, sliced, sautéed with 1.5 tsp soy, ¼ tsp sugar, ½ tsp garlic
- **Carrots** (2 large, batons) — sautéed 5-8 min
- **Zucchini** (2 large, batons) — sautéed 4 min
- **Spinach** (1 bunch) — sautéed with sesame oil until wilted
- **Bean sprouts** (4 cups) — boiled 5 min, seasoned with 2 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp garlic, ¼ tsp fish sauce

## Assembly
1. Place warm rice in bowls (short-grain preferred)
2. Arrange vegetables and beef with alternating colours
3. Top with sunny-side-up fried egg
4. Sprinkle sesame seeds, drizzle sesame oil
5. Serve with Bibimbap Sauce — mix everything before eating!

## Tips & Notes
- **Gochujang** — intense spicy miso paste, available at Asian grocery stores
- **Freeze beef** 30-60 min before slicing for paper-thin slices
- **Rice:** Short-grain or sushi rice is stickier
- **Excellent meal prep** — keeps 4-5 days

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~520 kcal
- Protein: 24g | Carbs: 68g | Fat: 16g

name: Australian Meat Pie
content:
# Australian Meat Pie

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/meat-pie-recipe/)
**Cuisine:** australian
**Type:** main
**Method:** baked
**Prep Time:** 30 min
**Cook Time:** 2 hrs 30 min
**Total Time:** ~4 hrs 20 min (plus cooling)
**Yield:** 6 servings

## Description
The great Australian Meat Pie! Shortcrust pastry filled with slow cooked chunky beef in rich gravy topped with golden puff pastry. Better than bakery pies!

## Ingredients

### Pie Base
- 1½ batches homemade shortcrust pastry OR 3 frozen shortcrust sheets (300g)

### Pie Lid
- 3 frozen puff pastry sheets (300g), just thawed
- 1 egg, lightly whisked (for egg wash)

### Filling
- 1.25 kg beef chuck, 2.5cm cubes (not gravy beef — too lean)
- Salt & pepper
- 2-3 tbsp olive oil
- 1 onion, diced
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 5 tbsp plain flour
- 1¼ cups (315ml) low-sodium beef stock
- 3 cups (750ml) dry full-bodied red wine (Shiraz, Cabernet, Merlot)
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
- 2 tsp coarsely ground black pepper
- 2 bay leaves

## Instructions
1. **Brown beef** in batches — deeply caramelized, do not crowd the pot
2. **Sauté** onion & garlic (3 mins), add flour, stir
3. **Add** stock, wine, tomato paste, Worcestershire, pepper, bay leaves
4. **Return beef**, cover, simmer gently 1 hr 45 mins
5. **Uncover**, simmer 30-45 mins until fork-tender and sauce thick
6. **Cool completely** (overnight is ideal)
7. **Blind bake** shortcrust base: 180°C, 20 mins with weights, 5 mins without
8. **Assemble:** Fill cooled pies, top with puff pastry, brush with egg, cut steam vent
9. **Bake** 30 mins until deep golden and puffed

## Tips & Notes
- **Brown beef aggressively** — builds deep flavour and color
- **Don't over-reduce sauce** — filling should be saucy, it thickens as it cools
- **Chill filling overnight** — prevents soggy base
- **Wine:** Use bold reds — Shiraz, Cabernet, Merlot
- **Always served with tomato sauce (ketchup)** — non-negotiable!
- **Freezer:** Freeze assembled (raw puff) or cooked pies

## Nutrition (per pie)
- Calories: 825 cal
- Protein: 46g | Carbs: 43g | Fat: 41g

name: Caramelised Vietnamese Shredded Beef
content:
# Caramelised Vietnamese Shredded Beef

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/caramelised-vietnamese-shredded-beef/)
**Cuisine:** vietnamese
**Type:** main
**Method:** stir-fry
**Prep Time:** 14 min
**Cook Time:** 6 min
**Total Time:** 20 min
**Yield:** 3-4 servings

## Description
Shredded beef tossed in a lemongrass sauce then pan fried until golden. Savoury, sweet and garlicky — those crispy golden bits are what we go bonkers over! Born as a way to use up leftover cooked brisket from making Pho.

## Ingredients

### Core
- 500g / 1lb (3 cups) leftover cooked brisket or other beef, shredded
- 1-2 tbsp oil for cooking

### Marinade
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp lime juice
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp soy sauce (light/all-purpose)
- 2.5 tbsp (packed) brown sugar
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 tbsp lemongrass paste (or 1 thick lemongrass stalk, very finely minced)

### Serving
- Rice or noodles
- Lime wedges OR Nuoc Cham dipping sauce
- Fresh coriander/cilantro and/or finely sliced green onions
- Finely sliced red chilli, sliced cucumber, crushed peanuts

## Instructions
1. **Mix Marinade** in a bowl. Add shredded beef and mix. Set aside 5 minutes.
2. **Heat oil** in a large skillet over high heat. Add about half the beef and spread out in single layer.
3. **Leave undisturbed** until golden (about 1 minute). Flip then cook the other side briefly (about 30 seconds). Remove.
4. **Repeat** with remaining beef.
5. **Serve** over rice with limes or Nuoc Cham, and garnishes.

## Tips & Notes
- **Leftover cooked beef** — ideal for using up brisket from Pho
- **To make from scratch:** Simmer brisket 3 hours with onion, garlic, ginger until shreddable
- **Nuoc Cham** — the garlic/chilli/lime/fish sauce sauce served with everything in Vietnamese cuisine
- **Great with:** Bean sprouts, fresh herbs (coriander, Thai basil, mint)

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~320 kcal
- Protein: 28g | Carbs: 18g | Fat: 14g

name: Moussaka (Greek Beef and Eggplant Bake)
content:
# Moussaka (Greek Beef and Eggplant Bake)

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/moussaka-greek-eggplant-beef-bake/)
**Cuisine:** greek
**Type:** main
**Method:** baked
**Prep Time:** 40 min
**Cook Time:** 30 min
**Total Time:** 1 hr 10 min
**Yield:** 6 servings

## Description
Moussaka is to the Greeks what Lasagna is to Italians. Layers of eggplant, rich spiced meat sauce, and thick béchamel topped with crunchy breadcrumbs. A low-carb alternative to lasagna.

## Ingredients

### Eggplant
- 1 kg (2 lb) eggplant, sliced 0.75cm thick
- 1 tsp salt (for sweating)

### Meat Sauce
- 700g (1.4 lb) ground beef or lamb
- 1 onion, diced
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- ½ cup dry red wine (optional)
- 14oz crushed tomatoes
- 3 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 cup beef broth
- 2 tsp dried oregano
- ½ tsp cinnamon
- 2 bay leaves
- 1.5 tsp sugar
- ¾ tsp salt

### Béchamel Sauce
- 4 tbsp butter
- 5 tbsp flour
- 2½ cups milk
- ½ cup grated Parmesan
- 1 egg + 1 egg yolk
- ¼ tsp nutmeg
- 1¼ tsp Vegeta/stock powder
- ¼ tsp pepper

### Assembly
- ⅓ cup panko breadcrumbs

## Instructions
1. **Prepare eggplant:** Sprinkle with salt, let sweat 30 mins. Bake at 240°C/450°F for 15-20 mins (or BBQ ~5 mins).
2. **Make meat sauce:** Sauté onion and garlic. Add meat, cook until browned. Add wine, tomatoes, paste, broth, and seasonings. Simmer until thick.
3. **Make béchamel:** Melt butter, whisk in flour, gradually add milk. Cook until thick. Remove from heat, stir in Parmesan, eggs, nutmeg, salt, pepper.
4. **Assemble:** Half eggplant → all meat sauce → remaining eggplant → béchamel → panko breadcrumbs
5. **Bake:** 180°C/350°F for 30-40 mins until golden
6. **Rest** 10 mins before serving

## Tips & Notes
- **Lamb** traditional, beef equally delicious
- **BBQ eggplant** praised for speed and even cooking
- **Panko breadcrumbs** preferred for superior crunch
- **Freezing:** Cool completely → wrap tightly → freeze. Reheat at 180°C for 25 mins
- **Dairy-free:** Works with oat milk

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: 434 cal
- Protein: 32g | Carbs: 20g | Fat: 24g

name: Beef Stroganoff
content:
# Beef Stroganoff

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/beef-stroganoff/)
**Cuisine:** russian
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 10 min
**Cook Time:** 15 min
**Total Time:** 25 min
**Yield:** 4 servings

## Description
Tender strips of beef in a creamy mushroom sauce. A classic comfort food that's elegant enough for company but easy enough for weeknights.

## Ingredients
- 600g / 1.3 lb beef steak (sirloin, rib eye, or tenderloin), sliced thinly against the grain
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- 1 large onion, diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 400g / 14 oz mushrooms (cremini or button), sliced
- 2 tbsp flour
- 1 cup (250ml) beef stock
- 1 cup (250ml) sour cream
- 2 tbsp Dijon mustard
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
- Salt and pepper
- Fresh parsley for garnish

## Instructions
1. **Season** beef with salt and pepper
2. **Sear** in hot butter/oil over high heat, 1-2 minutes until browned. Remove.
3. **Sauté** onion 3 mins, add garlic 30 sec, add mushrooms 5 mins until golden
4. **Sprinkle** flour over mushrooms, stir 1 minute
5. **Add** stock, mustard, Worcestershire. Simmer 3-4 minutes until slightly thickened
6. **Reduce heat** to low, stir in sour cream. Don't boil!
7. **Return** beef and juices to pan. Warm through gently.
8. **Serve** over egg noodles, rice, or mashed potatoes. Garnish with parsley.

## Tips & Notes
- **Don't overcook the beef** — it should be tender and pink inside
- **Don't boil** after adding sour cream — it may curdle
- **Best cuts:** Sirloin, rib eye, or tenderloin for quick cooking
- **Leftovers:** Keep 3 days in fridge. Reheat gently.

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~480 kcal
- Protein: 36g | Carbs: 14g | Fat: 28g

name: Asian Beef and Noodles
content:
# Asian Beef and Noodles

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/asian-beef-and-noodles/)
**Cuisine:** asian
**Type:** main
**Method:** stir-fry
**Prep Time:** 10 min
**Cook Time:** 8 min
**Total Time:** 18 min
**Yield:** 3-4 servings

## Description
Quick Asian-style beef with noodles in a savoury-sweet sauce. Caramelised beef bits cling to noodles for maximum flavour in every bite.

## Ingredients

### Beef & Marinade
- 400g / 14 oz beef mince (ground beef)
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tsp sesame oil
- 1 tsp cornflour

### Noodles & Veg
- 200g / 7 oz fresh noodles (or 150g dried)
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp grated ginger
- 1 red capsicum (bell pepper), sliced
- 1 cup broccoli florets
- 2 green onions, sliced

### Sauce
- 3 tbsp soy sauce (light/all-purpose)
- 2 tbsp oyster sauce
- 1 tbsp hoisin sauce
- 1 tbsp rice vinegar
- 1 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tsp sriracha (optional)
- ½ cup water
- 1 tsp cornflour (slurry)

## Instructions
1. **Mix marinade** with beef; set aside 5 minutes
2. **Cook noodles** per packet directions; drain
3. **Mix sauce** ingredients; set aside
4. **Stir fry** — Heat oil, add garlic and ginger 10 sec. Add beef, cook breaking up until browned and caramelised.
5. **Add vegetables** — Capsicum and broccoli, stir fry 1-2 minutes
6. **Add noodles and sauce** — Toss everything together, cook 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens
7. **Garnish** with green onions, serve immediately

## Tips & Notes
- **Caramelisation is key** — let the beef brown well before stirring
- **Noodle types:** Hokkien, udon, ramen, or rice noodles all work
- **Vegetable swaps:** Snow peas, bean sprouts, carrots
- **Best eaten fresh** — noodles absorb sauce over time

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~480 kcal
- Protein: 30g | Carbs: 42g | Fat: 22g

name: Braised Beef Short Ribs in Red Wine Sauce
content:
# Braised Beef Short Ribs in Red Wine Sauce

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/braised-beef-short-ribs-in-red-wine-sauce/)
**Cuisine:** french
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 20 min
**Cook Time:** 2 hrs 30 min
**Total Time:** ~3 hrs
**Yield:** 4-6 servings

## Description
Fall-apart tender beef short ribs braised in a rich red wine sauce with aromatics. A luxurious dish perfect for special occasions.

## Ingredients
- 1.5 kg / 3.3 lb beef short ribs (bone-in or boneless)
- Salt and pepper
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 large onion, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 carrots, diced
- 2 celery stalks, diced
- 2 cups (500ml) dry red wine
- 1 cup (250ml) beef stock
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 3 sprigs fresh thyme
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar

## Instructions
1. **Season** short ribs generously with salt and pepper
2. **Sear** in hot oil over high heat until deeply browned on all sides. Remove.
3. **Sauté aromatics** — Cook onion and garlic 3 mins. Add carrots and celery, cook 5 mins.
4. **Deglaze** — Add red wine, scrape up brown bits
5. **Combine** — Add beef stock, tomato paste, thyme, bay leaves. Return beef.
6. **Braise** — Cover, simmer gently for 2-2.5 hours until fall-apart tender
7. **Finish** — Remove beef, strain sauce if desired. Stir in balsamic vinegar.
8. **Serve** over creamy mashed potatoes or polenta

## Tips & Notes
- **Searing is essential** — builds the deep flavour base
- **Red wine** — use a good quality dry red (Bordeaux, Cabernet, Merlot)
- **Make ahead:** Tastes even better the next day
- **Slow cooker:** Cook on LOW for 8 hours after searing
- **Freezes well** for up to 3 months

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~580 kcal
- Protein: 48g | Carbs: 12g | Fat: 30g

name: Marinated Beef Kabobs
content:
# Marinated Beef Kabobs

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/marinated-beef-kabobs/)
**Cuisine:** mediterranean
**Type:** main
**Method:** grilled
**Prep Time:** 20 min
**Cook Time:** 10 min
**Total Time:** ~30 min (plus marinating)
**Yield:** 4-6 servings

## Description
Tender marinated beef cubes grilled on skewers with vegetables. A Mediterranean classic perfect for summer barbecues.

## Ingredients

### Marinade
- ¼ cup (65 ml) olive oil
- 3 tbsp lemon juice
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp dried oregano
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp paprika
- ½ tsp cinnamon
- 1 tsp salt
- ½ tsp black pepper

### Kabobs
- 800g / 1.8 lb beef sirloin or rib eye, cut into 2.5cm cubes
- 2 bell peppers (red and green), cut into 2.5cm squares
- 1 large red onion, cut into 2.5cm squares
- 200g / 7 oz button mushrooms
- Wooden or metal skewers

## Instructions
1. **Mix marinade** ingredients in a large bowl or ziplock bag
2. **Add beef**, marinate 2-24 hours in the fridge
3. **Thread** beef and vegetables onto skewers, alternating
4. **Grill** over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, 8-10 minutes for medium-rare
5. **Rest** 3 minutes before serving

## Tips & Notes
- **Don't overcrowd** skewers — leave small gaps for even cooking
- **Soak wooden skewers** in water 30 minutes before using
- **Vegetable swaps:** Zucchini, cherry tomatoes, eggplant
- **Serve with:** Tzatziki, hummus, pita bread, Greek salad

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~380 kcal
- Protein: 34g | Carbs: 10g | Fat: 22g

name: Massaman Curry
content:
# Massaman Curry

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/massaman-curry/)
**Cuisine:** thai
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 15 min
**Cook Time:** 2 hrs
**Total Time:** 2 hrs 15 min
**Yield:** 4-6 servings

## Description
Thailand's most beloved curry — rich, creamy, and mildly spiced with potatoes, peanuts, and warm spices. A comforting one-pot meal.

## Ingredients

### Massaman Curry Paste (or use store-bought)
- 3 dried red chillies, rehydrated
- 1 stalk lemongrass, white part only
- 1 tbsp galangal, chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger, chopped
- 3 cloves garlic
- 1 shallot
- 1 tsp ground coriander
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- ½ tsp ground cinnamon
- ¼ tsp ground cloves
- ¼ tsp ground cardamom

### Curry
- 800g / 1.8 lb beef chuck, cut into 3cm cubes
- 2 tbsp oil
- 400ml / 14 oz coconut milk
- 1 cup (250ml) beef stock
- 2 medium potatoes, peeled and quartered
- 1 large onion, cut into wedges
- ¼ cup roasted peanuts
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tbsp tamarind paste
- 2 kaffir lime leaves

## Instructions
1. **Make curry paste** — Blend all paste ingredients until smooth
2. **Brown beef** in hot oil in batches. Remove.
3. **Fry curry paste** — Cook in same oil 2-3 minutes until fragrant
4. **Add coconut milk** — Stir until oil separates from cream
5. **Return beef** — Add stock, potatoes, onion, peanuts, fish sauce, sugar, tamarind, lime leaves
6. **Simmer** covered for 1.5-2 hours until beef is tender and potatoes are cooked
7. **Adjust seasoning** — Add more fish sauce (salt), sugar (sweet), or tamarind (sour) to taste
8. **Serve** over jasmine rice, garnish with extra peanuts and fresh chilli

## Tips & Notes
- **Store-bought paste** works well — Mae Ploy or Maesri brands recommended
- **Mildly spiced** — one of the mildest Thai curries
- **Make ahead:** Tastes even better the next day
- **Freezes well** for up to 3 months

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~520 kcal
- Protein: 36g | Carbs: 24g | Fat: 32g

name: Peppercorn Sauce
content:
# Peppercorn Sauce

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/peppercorn-sauce/)
**Cuisine:** french
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 5 min
**Cook Time:** 10 min
**Total Time:** 15 min
**Yield:** 4 servings

## Description
A classic French peppercorn sauce — creamy, peppery, and luxurious. Perfect with steak, chicken, or pork. The same sauce used for Steak au Poivre.

## Ingredients
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 shallot, finely diced
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp green peppercorns (in brine, drained) — or 1 tbsp black peppercorns, coarsely crushed
- ¼ cup (60ml) brandy or cognac (or beef stock)
- 1 cup (250ml) beef stock
- ½ cup (125ml) heavy cream
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- Salt to taste

## Instructions
1. **Sauté** shallot in butter over medium heat until soft (2 mins)
2. **Add** garlic and peppercorns, cook 30 seconds
3. **Deglaze** with brandy — let it bubble and reduce by half (or use stock)
4. **Add** beef stock, simmer 3-4 minutes until reduced by half
5. **Stir in** cream and Dijon mustard, simmer 2-3 minutes until thickened
6. **Season** with salt to taste
7. **Serve** immediately over steak, chicken, or pork

## Tips & Notes
- **Green peppercorns** are traditional — milder and more aromatic than black
- **Brandy/cognac** adds depth but can be substituted with extra beef stock
- **Don't boil** after adding cream — it may curdle
- **Pairs with:** Grilled steak, roast chicken, pan-seared pork chops

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~220 kcal
- Protein: 4g | Carbs: 6g | Fat: 18g

name: Salisbury Steak with Mushroom Gravy
content:
# Salisbury Steak with Mushroom Gravy

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/salisbury-steak-with-mushroom-gravy/)
**Cuisine:** american
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 15 min
**Cook Time:** 20 min
**Total Time:** 35 min
**Yield:** 4 servings

## Description
Juicy beef patties smothered in rich mushroom gravy. An American diner classic that's pure comfort food.

## Ingredients

### Patties
- 500g / 1 lb ground beef (mince)
- 1 small onion, grated
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 1 egg
- ¼ cup breadcrumbs
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- Salt and pepper
- 1 tbsp olive oil (for frying)

### Mushroom Gravy
- 2 tbsp butter
- 250g / 9 oz mushrooms, sliced
- 1 small onion, diced
- 2 tbsp flour
- 1½ cups (375ml) beef stock
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- Salt and pepper

## Instructions
1. **Make patties** — Mix all patty ingredients (except oil). Form into 4 oval patties.
2. **Cook patties** — Heat oil in skillet over medium-high. Cook patties 4-5 min per side until browned. Remove.
3. **Make gravy** — In same pan, melt butter. Sauté mushrooms and onion 5 mins until golden.
4. **Thicken** — Sprinkle flour over mushrooms, stir 1 minute.
5. **Add liquids** — Gradually add stock, Worcestershire, and mustard. Simmer 3-4 minutes until thickened.
6. **Combine** — Return patties to gravy, simmer 5 minutes until cooked through.
7. **Serve** over mashed potatoes or egg noodles.

## Tips & Notes
- **Grated onion** keeps the patties moist
- **Don't overwork** the meat mixture — it makes tough patties
- **Make ahead:** Gravy can be made 2 days ahead

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~420 kcal
- Protein: 32g | Carbs: 14g | Fat: 24g

name: Osso Buco
content:
# Osso Buco

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/osso-buco/)
**Cuisine:** italian
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 15 min
**Cook Time:** 1 hr 30 min
**Total Time:** 1 hr 45 min
**Yield:** 5 servings

## Description
Fork tender veal braised in a rich red sauce. Osso Buco is a meal fit for a king! The prized sticky bone marrow inside the shank is a highlight.

## Ingredients

### Main
- 5 thick veal osso buco steaks (300g / 10oz each, 2.5cm / 1" thick) — or beef
- Salt and pepper
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 small onion, finely chopped
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 small carrot, finely chopped
- 1 celery stick, finely chopped
- 800g / 28oz can crushed tomato
- 1 cup / 250ml dry white wine (not sweet/fruity)
- 1 cup / 250ml chicken broth
- 1 chicken bouillon cube (optional)
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 3 sprigs thyme (or 1½ tsp dried)
- 2 bay leaves

### Gremolata
- 1 cup parsley leaves, lightly packed
- 2-3 tsp lemon zest, finely grated
- 1 garlic clove, minced

## Instructions
1. **Season** veal with salt and pepper
2. **Sear** in hot olive oil over high heat until browned on both sides. Remove.
3. **Sauté aromatics** — Cook garlic and onion 1 minute, then carrot and celery 8 minutes until softened
4. **Build sauce** — Add crushed tomato, wine, broth, bouillon, tomato paste, thyme, bay leaves
5. **Return veal**, cover, adjust to gentle simmer
6. **Braise** 1.5-2 hours until fork-tender
7. **Remove veal**, simmer sauce 5-15 minutes to thicken
8. **Make gremolata** — Finely chop parsley, garlic, and lemon zest together. Set aside 20 minutes.
9. **Serve** — Place osso buco on plate, spoon over sauce, top with gremolata

## Tips & Notes
- **String trick:** Tie kitchen string around veal AFTER browning, not before
- **Veal cooks faster** than beef: no need to braise beyond 2 hours
- **Gremolata is essential** — provides critical contrast to rich sauce
- **Bone marrow** is the prized highlight
- **Traditional serving:** Saffron Risotto (Risotto Milanese)
- **Slow cooker:** Low for 4-6 hours after searing
- **Instant Pot:** 30 min pressure cook + 15 min simmer to reduce

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: 549 cal
- Protein: 42g | Carbs: 18g | Fat: 28g

name: Satay Peanut Stir Fry
content:
# Satay Peanut Stir Fry

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/satay-peanut-stir-fry/)
**Cuisine:** asian
**Type:** main
**Method:** stir-fry
**Prep Time:** 10 min
**Cook Time:** 7 min
**Total Time:** 17 min
**Yield:** 3-4 servings

## Description
A fast, flavorful stir fry inspired by Chinese restaurant-style satay. Made with a simple master peanut sauce — works with any protein.

## Ingredients

### Sauce
- 1½ tbsp fish sauce
- 1½ tsp sugar
- 2 tsp cornflour / cornstarch
- 1 garlic clove, minced
- 1 tsp grated ginger
- 1 tsp curry powder
- 1½ tbsp crunchy peanut butter
- 2 tbsp water

### Stir Fry
- 300-350g / 10-12 oz beef, thinly sliced
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 garlic clove, finely chopped
- 1 large onion, sliced
- 1 large carrot, julienned
- 1 big handful bean sprouts (~1½ cups)
- ½ cup finely sliced shallots / green onions
- ¼ cup crushed peanuts (optional)

## Instructions
1. **Mix sauce** — Combine all sauce ingredients except water, then stir in water
2. **Marinate protein** — Add 1 tbsp sauce to beef, set aside 10 minutes
3. **Cook aromatics** — Heat oil on high. Add garlic, stir until golden (~10 sec), add onion. Cook 1 minute.
4. **Sear protein** — Add beef, cook 1½ minutes until mostly browned
5. **Add veggies & sauce** — Add carrots, bean sprouts, and remaining sauce. Cook until vegetables wilted.
6. **Finish** — Stir in crushed peanuts and shallots
7. **Serve** immediately with rice or noodles

## Tips & Notes
- **Protein flexibility:** Works with chicken, pork, prawns, or tofu
- **Fish sauce substitute:** Use light/all-purpose soy sauce
- **Velveting tip:** For ultra-tender meat, use velveting technique
- **Noodle variation:** Double the sauce and add 350g noodles

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: 363 kcal
- Protein: 24g | Carbs: 18g | Fat: 20g

name: Epic Chunky Beef and Mushroom Pie
content:
# Epic Chunky Beef and Mushroom Pie

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/epic-chunky-beef-and-mushroom-pie/)
**Cuisine:** british
**Type:** main
**Method:** baked
**Prep Time:** 15 min
**Cook Time:** 2 hrs 30 min
**Total Time:** 2 hrs 45 min
**Yield:** 6 servings

## Description
The secret ingredient: **Dried Porcini Mushrooms** — just 20g adds a powerful punch of savory, earthy umami that elevates this pie beyond the ordinary.

## Ingredients

### Core
- 1 kg beef chuck (or brisket/gravy beef), cut into 2.5cm cubes
- 20g dried porcini mushrooms
- 300-400g Swiss brown mushrooms
- 200g diced bacon
- 1 onion, diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 carrot, finely chopped + 1 carrot, chunked
- 1 celery, finely chopped
- 300ml dark ale (or red wine, Guinness, or extra beef stock)
- 2 cups beef stock
- 3 sprigs thyme
- 2 bay leaves
- Flour, puff pastry, egg yolk (for glaze)

## Instructions
1. **Soak porcini** in 1¼ cups boiled water for ≥30 mins. Strain, chop, reserve liquid.
2. **Brown beef** in batches; set aside.
3. **Sauté aromatics:** Onion & garlic (2 mins), finely chopped carrot & celery (6 mins), chunked carrot (2 mins).
4. **Add flour**, stir to coat.
5. **Deglaze** with ale, then add stock, herbs, porcini, soaking liquid, and beef.
6. **Simmer covered** for 1 hr 45 mins until beef is tender.
7. **Cook bacon** until golden; sauté mushrooms in same pan. Combine with bacon, stir into stew.
8. **Simmer uncovered** 15 mins. Cool slightly.
9. **Assemble:** Pour into pie dish. Top with puff pastry, brush with egg yolk, cut slits.
10. **Bake** at 200°C/390°F for 30-35 mins until golden.
11. **Rest 5 mins** before serving.

## Tips & Notes
- **Porcini substitution:** Essential for umami depth, but pie is still excellent without
- **Slow cooker:** Low 6 hrs or high 3 hrs after initial steps
- **Pressure cooker:** High 25 mins after step 7
- **Make ahead:** Filling tastes better next day — refrigerate overnight
- **Freezer:** Filling freezes well; can freeze with raw pastry on top

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~650 kcal
- Protein: 42g | Carbs: 38g | Fat: 32g

name: Carne Asada
content:
# Carne Asada

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/carne-asada/)
**Cuisine:** mexican
**Type:** main
**Method:** grilled
**Prep Time:** 10 min
**Cook Time:** 8 min
**Total Time:** 18 min
**Yield:** 6 servings

## Description
"Carne Asada" means "grilled beef" in Spanish. A citrusy, garlicky, coriander-forward marinade that both flavors and tenderizes the meat. Epic Latin American beef!

## Ingredients

### Marinade
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- ½ cup / 125 ml orange juice
- ¼ cup / 65 ml lime juice (~2 limes)
- 1 cup finely chopped coriander/cilantro
- ½ teaspoon salt
- ¼ teaspoon black pepper
- ¼ cup / 65 ml olive oil
- 1 jalapeño (fresh), finely minced
- 2 tbsp white vinegar

### Cooking
- Oil (for grill/griddle)
- 750 g – 1.2 kg / 1.5 – 2.5 lb skirt or flank steak

### To Serve as Tacos
- Tortillas, avocado slices, Pico de Gallo, coriander, lime wedges

## Instructions
1. **Marinate:** Place marinade ingredients in a ziplock bag, add beef, massage to coat. Marinate **1-8 hours** (max 12 hours).
2. **Temper:** Remove beef from fridge 20-30 minutes before cooking.
3. **Heat:** Brush BBQ or large skillet with oil; heat on high until smoking.
4. **Cook:**
   - Skirt steak: 3 min/side (rare), 4 min/side (medium rare)
   - Flank steak: 4 min/side (rare), 5 min/side (medium rare)
   - Do not cook beyond medium — it gets chewy.
5. **Rest:** 5 minutes under loose foil.
6. **Slice:** Cut thinly against the grain (90° to meat fibers).
7. **Serve:** Tacos, burritos, rice, or salad.

## Tips & Notes
- **Best cuts:** Skirt and flank steak — secondary cuts with stronger beef flavor
- **Don't overcook!** Err on the side of undercooking
- **Other proteins:** Chicken (12-24 hrs), pork tenderloin (3-12 hrs), fish/prawns (15 min only)
- **Cutting against the grain:** Look for direction of meat fibers, cut 90° across them

## Nutrition (per serving, 1 kg beef)
- Calories: ~350 kcal
- Protein: 38g | Carbs: 4g | Fat: 18g

name: Beef Rendang
content:
# Beef Rendang

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/beef-rendang/)
**Cuisine:** malaysian
**Type:** main
**Method:** stovetop
**Prep Time:** 20 min
**Cook Time:** 3 hrs
**Total Time:** 3 hrs 20 min
**Yield:** 6 servings

## Description
The KING OF ALL CURRIES! Beef Rendang has incredible depth of flavour, with complexity and many layers of spices. A dry curry — rich, thick sauce coats tender beef.

## Ingredients

### Spice Paste
- 12 dried chilies (rehydrated) or 12 fresh long red chilies
- 1 small onion, finely chopped
- 5 cloves garlic, minced
- 3 lemongrass stalks, white part only, sliced
- 1½ tbsp fresh galangal, finely chopped
- 1½ tbsp fresh ginger, minced
- 2 tbsp oil

### Curry
- 2 lb (1 kg) chuck steak, cut into 4cm cubes
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 cinnamon stick
- ¼ tsp clove powder
- 3 star anise
- ½ tsp cardamom powder
- 1 lemongrass stick (bottom half, smashed)
- 400ml (14 oz) coconut milk
- 2 tsp tamarind puree/paste
- 4 large kaffir lime leaves, very finely sliced
- ⅓ cup desiccated (finely shredded) coconut
- 1 tbsp brown sugar
- 1½ tsp salt

## Instructions
1. **Make spice paste:** Blend all spice paste ingredients until fine.
2. **Brown beef:** Heat oil over high heat. Brown half the beef, remove, repeat.
3. **Cook paste:** Lower heat to medium-low. Cook spice paste 2-3 minutes until darkened and fragrant.
4. **Combine & simmer:** Add all curry ingredients + beef. Stir, bring to simmer, reduce heat to low. Cover and cook 1 hr 15 mins.
5. **Reduce sauce:** Increase heat to medium. Uncover and reduce for 30-40 mins, stirring frequently, until sauce becomes a thick paste and oil separates.
6. **Serve** with coconut rice or plain rice.

## Tips & Notes
- **Chilies:** 12 = moderately spicy. Use 6 for mild.
- **Beef cuts:** Chuck (best), gravy beef, beef cheeks, or brisket
- **Kaffir lime leaves:** Fresh strongly recommended (freeze well)
- **Improves with time** — ideal made 1-2 days ahead
- **Freezes well**
- **Slow cooker:** Low 6 hrs, then finish reduction on stove
- **Pressure cooker:** High 30 mins, natural release, then reduce on stove

## Nutrition (per serving)
- Calories: ~480 kcal
- Protein: 38g | Carbs: 14g | Fat: 30g
